description_long: This object is a carved stone guard stone featuring a kneeling human figure integrated into its structure, with the figure's arms resting on a vertical slab. The slab is inscribed with multiple rows of hieroglyphic text, suggesting an ancient Egyptian origin or influence. The figure has a stylized, elongated head and detailed facial features, with the body partially emerging from the stone base. The stone is dark, likely basalt or granite, with a weathered, mottled surface showing signs of age. The rear of the object is less detailed, with some additional inscriptions running vertically along the back of the slab. The base is rectangular and robust, providing stability and a sense of permanence.
description: A dark stone guard stone with a kneeling human figure and inscribed hieroglyphs on a vertical slab, showing weathered and ancient features.
description_view_0: Front view shows the kneeling figure facing forward, arms on the inscribed slab with hieroglyphs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the profile of the kneeling figure and the thickness of the inscribed slab.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the figure and vertical inscriptions along the slab's rear edge.
description_view_3: Right side view displays the figure's profile and the stone's mottled texture, with the slab's thickness evident.
attribute_location: Typically found at the entrance to historical sites, temples, or as a boundary marker in urban or archaeological settings, often positioned near pathways or at the edge of plazas.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright on its rectangular base for maximum visibility and stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Surface shows signs of weathering, with some smoothing and rounding of edges, minor chipping at the base, and discoloration likely due to prolonged exposure to the elements and possible contact with passing objects or foot traffic.
attribute_historical_markings: Multiple rows of hieroglyphic inscriptions on the front slab and additional vertical inscriptions on the rear edge, all carved in a traditional ancient Egyptian style.
